title: Branches, Integration, and Merge Evidence · Recovery Case: Switch Worktree State
summary: Integrate a short-lived change while preserving understandable topology and verifying both file content and combined behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git switch worktree state increment in a branch-and-merge topology lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Switch Worktree State while producing a branch-and-merge topology lab.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. Competency probe: Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints. A defensible solution applies Switch Worktree State, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a branch-and-merge topology lab.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-switch-worktree-state — Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
